title: centaurea boissieri subsp. calvescens
intro: Centaurea boissieri subsp. calvescens, also known as mountain cornflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Centaurea boissieri subsp. calvescens include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and even serve as an antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
